business.headline: Retail inflation rises to 3.40% in March from 3.21% in FebruaryThe year-on-year inflation rate, based on All India Consumer Price Index (CPI), stood at 3.40% (Provisional) in March 2026. This is slightly higher than February's 3.21%, but still below the 4% mark that is often seen as manageable.

8th Pay Commission meetings: Should govt employees expect a pay revision soon?After months of speculation, the 8th Pay Commission process has picked up pace. With meetings now scheduled, the big question remains, i.e., should government employees expect a pay revision anytime soon?

Sensex ends 700 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; auto stocks hit hardMarkets may have steadied after early losses, but rising oil prices and geopolitical risks continue to cloud sentiment, keeping investors cautious and leaving the near-term outlook uncertain and volatile.

Maruti, Eicher Motors, other auto stocks tumble: What's behind the fall?Maruti Suzuki fell over 4%, while Eicher Motors dropped nearly 5%, making them among the top losers on the Nifty 50. Other auto names also saw weakness, even as the broader market pared some of its early losses.

Ambanis lead Asia's 10 richest families in 2026: Check who else is on the listAt number one is the Ambani family, with a net worth of about $89.7 billion. Their flagship company, Reliance Industries, continues to expand across energy, telecom, retail and financial services.

When employers evade, enforcement looks awaySystemic gaps in enforcement, coupled with deliberate non-compliance, leave workers outside legal protection, raising a larger question: whether labour laws exist merely on paper or are meant to be enforced.

New labour codes explained: How your salary, PF, and gratuity will changeEmployees should ask four key questions: What's my actual wage base? Is PF on capped or full wages? Is this just a salary split change? And am I gaining long-term benefits?

Sensex pares losses by more than 800 points, Nifty recovers from early crashStock market crash: The recovery follows a steep fall at the open, when the Sensex had plunged over 1,600 points and the Nifty slipped below key levels in early trade, reflecting a weak start to the week.

DA hike coming: How much will your monthly pay rise-Rs 360, Rs 540 or more?As the DA hike nears, expectations are building. Even a modest increase can add a steady boost to your salary, but how much can you really expect this time? Let's have a look.

Jyoti CNC shares tumble up to 16%: What's behind the sharp fall?The sharp fall follows reports that French authorities have initiated a judicial probe into the company's subsidiary, Huron Graffenstaden SAS, over alleged violations linked to export control norms.

Stagflation fears are back: How should investors position their money in 2026?During stagflation, money gets re-allocated in the market hence few of the asset classes which usually do not come across as attractive options may become the flavour of the season.

US plans Hormuz blockade: Will India's crude oil and LPG supply be hit?India is already facing pressure on fuel and LPG supplies amid the West Asia conflict. With the US now planning a Hormuz blockade, could the situation worsen?

Sensex tumbles 1,600 points: What you should know about today's market crashMarkets are reacting to oil above $100 and rising Hormuz tensions. If the US blockade begins and shipping risks escalate, banks, the rupee and foreign flows could face sustained pressure.

Sensex tumbles 1,600 points as US Hormuz blockade threat pushes crude above $100Stock market crash: The main indices on Dalal Street opened sharply lower as the Sensex and Nifty crashed ahead of a US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, with crude oil already surging above $100 per barrel.

Forgotten fortune: Why assets worth Rs 2 lakh crore remain unclaimed in IndiaUnclaimed assets across bank deposits, shares, insurance policies and retirement funds have crossed Rs 2 lakh crore. This article explains why this money remains out of reach and what makes it so hard to recover.

Buying a car or bike? The hidden truth behind insurance sold at dealershipsWhen you buy a car or bike, the final bill usually comes as an 'on-road price' that includes insurance. For most buyers, this is accepted without much thought, as it appears to be a standard part of the purchase. But what often goes unnoticed is that this insurance is not mandatory to buy from the dealer.

Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

India allows 4 sanctioned tankers carrying Iranian oil to berth at Sikka: ReportIranian oil is often transported by a so-called shadow fleet of vessels that lack internationally recognised insurance and safety certifications. But this requires special permission from the government as exemptions are required under Indian rules for the berthing of ships.

Gold dips, silver slips: Is this the right time to invest or wait?Prices of gold and silver have cooled off after recent highs, but the bigger question remains, is this the right entry point, or is there more downside ahead?

Why April is the best time to reset your budget, SIPs and other investmentsIn Part 2 of our MONEY MAKEOVER series, we explore how April, with salary hikes, bonuses and tax changes, creates the perfect window to pause, reset and move forward with clarity. Let's have a look.

entertainment.headline: Asha Bhosle once revealed her last wish, and it sums up her 83 years in music Asha Bhosle once shared a deeply personal wish about how she wanted to say goodbye, a sentiment that now feels especially poignant. As the legendary voice falls silent at 92, her words offer a moving glimpse into a life devoted entirely to music.

Left us an entire encyclopedia of eternal music: Big B's tribute to Asha Bhosle Amitabh Bachchan led tributes to Asha Bhosle, calling her death the end of an era. The legendary singer died at the age of 92.

Saaz, the film that triggered talk around Asha Bhosle's bond with Lata didi Sai Paranjpye's 1998 film, Saaz, traced the relationship between two sisters who become successful singers. Were Aruna Irani and Shabana Azmi's roles inspired by Lata Mangeshkar and Asha Bhosle in any way?

Ilaiyaraaja calls Asha Bhosle sister in video tribute: Great talent, unique voice Veteran music composer Ilaiyaraaja expressed deep grief over the loss of iconic singer Asha Bhosle. He fondly remembered their collaborations and prayed for her lasting legacy.

Dhurandhar's Aditya Uppal recalls watching Sanjay Dutt on VCR, then acting with him Actor Aditya Uppal recalled watching Sanjay Dutt on VCR and shared his experience of acting with him in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He also named Farhan Akhtar as his dream collaborator.

Jr NTR flexes his muscles, stuns fans with new pic ahead of NTRNeel shoot Jr NTR's toned physique for his upcoming film, tentatively titled Dragon, has sparked widespread excitement among fans and the film fraternity. The makers have clarified there are no delays in shooting, with the project progressing as planned.

Actor Kasthuri admits watching leaked Jana Nayagan clips: 3-hour propaganda for TVK Actor Kasthuri Shankar admitted to viewing leaked clips of Jana Nayagan, labelling it a political propaganda film supporting Vijay's party, TVK. The leak had sparked controversy just before the Tamil Nadu assembly elections.

Ramayana heads to CinemaCon 2026: Yash to attend, posters stir buzz online The upcoming film Ramayana is gearing up for a major showcase at CinemaCon 2026 in Las Vegas, with Yash expected to be in attendance. Several photos of the film's posters displayed at the venue have surfaced online.

Dhurandhar 2's 25-day run: Biggest Indian grosser excluding China, Gulf box office Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is rewriting box office rules, emerging as the top Indian grosser when China and Gulf earnings are taken out of the equation. As the Ranveer Singh-starrer storms past Rs 1,700 crore worldwide, its unprecedented run signals a major shift in how global success is defined for Indian cinema.

Perfect Crown first impressions: All glitter, little gold Perfect Crown, starring IU and Byeon Woo-seok, blends royal intrigue with rom-com elements but needs stronger writing to engage fully, says our review.

Euphoria 3 puts OnlyFans debate in focus: Cast members say 'not for us to judge' Euphoria returns with its third season, delving into the complexities of digital-age sex work and OnlyFans culture. At a global roundtable, actors Martha Kelly and Chloe Cherry talked about OnlyFans, whether it is empowering or exploiting women.

Rajinikanth's film to undergo another change: Is it Ashwath Marimuthu over Cibi? Rajinikanth's highly awaited film Thalaivar 173 is facing uncertainty as director Cibi Chakravarthi has reportedly exited the project. Fans and industry insiders await official confirmation and updates on the film's future direction.

Kareena Kapoor skips queue at airport. Security drill or celebrity perk? A viral video showing Kareena Kapoor Khan bypassing a long airport queue has sparked a heated debate online. Security protocol or star privilege, either way, Bebo's airport walk just skipped straight into controversy.

State funeral for Asha Bhosle in Mumbai; fans to pay last respects at residence The death of Asha Bhosle marks the end of a defining voice in Indian music. As the nation prepares to bid farewell with state honours in Mumbai, here's a look at the final journey of the legendary singer.

Jana Nayagan leak: 6 people arrested, 300 links removed in cyber wing crackdown The Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested six individuals for leaking the movie Jana Nayagan online in high-definition. Authorities continue to block over 300 infringing links and warn against piracy.

Britney Spears goes to rehab after arrest in driving under influence case Britney Spears has reportedly checked into a treatment facility following her arrest for driving under the influence. The pop star is said to have entered a voluntary rehab programme amid legal pressure and growing concern from her inner circle.

Video: Asha Bhosle's last public outing was at Arjun Tendulkar's wedding Singer Asha Bhosle's last public appearance was at Arjun Tendulkar's wedding in Mumbai in March. The legendary singer died on April 12, leaving behind a remarkable legacy in Indian music.

Throwback pics show Sonu Nigam washing Asha Bhosle's feet, bowing in respect Sonu Nigam honoured Asha Bhosle's legacy with heartfelt memories and rare photos. He recalled their decades-long journey and remembered their first meeting in 1994.

Katy Perry-Justin Trudeau enjoy at Coachella, her joke on Bieber steals spotlight Katy Perry and Justin Trudeau shared candid moments at Coachella with a playful remark on Justin Bieber's set. The couple's photos and videos from the date night are now taking social media by a storm.

general news.headline: No Gulf port will be safe: Iran's big warning hours before US blockade beginsIran warned that no port in the Persian Gulf or Sea of Oman would remain safe if its own ports are targeted, as the US prepares to enforce a blockade on Iranian maritime activity. The move follows failed talks, with tensions escalating, shipping disrupted in the Strait of Hormuz, and oil prices surging amid fears of wider regional fallout.

Did team leads run religious conversion racket, prey on women at TCS Nashik?Allegations of sexual exploitation and forced religious conversion have surfaced from IT major TCS's Nashik facility. Multiple FIRs have been lodged, and several people, including team leads and engineers, have been arrested. The alleged harassment is said to have continued for two to three years.

Vehicles torched, stones thrown, long jam: Workers' protest in Noida turns violent  Live UpdatesLarge groups blocked key roads in Noida, triggering massive traffic jams, while police were forced to use mild force to disperse the protesters.

f(f(O))>f(O): What does Iran's mathematical warning to US over Hormuz mean?In a post on X, Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf mocked the US's warning of a naval blockade on ships linked to Iranian ports, suggesting the move could ultimately rebound on American consumers by driving up fuel costs.

Three scenarios as a planned US Hormuz blockade risks huge global falloutThe US's move to control the Strait of Hormuz risks bringing in major powers and disrupting the fragile balance of energy flows.

We can't be blinded by elections: SC backs voters' right to be on rolls amid SIRThe Supreme Court flagged concerns over the Special Intensive Review (SIR) of electoral roll revision in poll-bound West Bengal, asserting that voters have a continuing right to be on the election roll.

Netanyahu's call to Vance derailed US-Iran talks? Araghchi reveals inside detailsIran-US talks in Pakistan ended without agreement after 21 hours, with Tehran alleging that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's phone call to JD Vance in Islamabad disrupted negotiations.

Live: Noida firm says workers' protest driven by misinformation about wagesNoida Labour Protest Live Updates: A wage protest by labour unions turned violent in Noida on Monday morning, with workers torching vehicles, clashing with police, blocking roads and vandalising factories, triggering massive traffic jams across Noida and parts of Delhi, leaving commuters stranded during peak office hours. Authorities deployed additional forces to contain the unrest as workers from multiple industrial units pressed for long-pending salary hikes. Commuters faced delays reaching schools and offices, with autos and cabs scarce or charging nearly triple fares. Similar protests were seen in Faridabad, involving Motherson Group staffers, and spread to parts of Rajasthan, including Bhiwadi, where factory workers demanded higher wages. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

Trump posts AI image of himself as Jesus Christ amid feud with Pope LeoUS President Donald Trump shared a Christ-like image of himself after launching a sharp attack on Pope Leo XIV, calling him "weak" and "very liberal" over his stance on global conflicts.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Left us an entire encyclopedia of eternal music: Big B's tribute to Asha BhosleAmitabh Bachchan led tributes to Asha Bhosle, calling her death the end of an era. The legendary singer died at the age of 92.

Hindi books event in US cancelled after protests over VHP links, Hindutva spreadA coalition of civil rights and religious groups forced the Montgomery County Public Libraries to cancel its Hindi book celebration event, saying it was linked to the Vishwa Hindu Parishad's US affiliate VHPA, which they said spread "Hindutva" ideology. The VHPA called it "misinformation campaign" by "Hinduphobic groups".

After DMK's royal snub, what prompted Udhayanidhi Stalin's jibe at VijayThe DMK's calibrated strategy of sidelining Vijay has given way to a more combative posture, with Udhayanidhi Stalin leading the charge on the ground.

From Gurugram to Noida, how violent wage protests spread across NCRVehicles were torched, property was vandalised and stone pelting was reported in Noida on Monday as protests by factory workers demanding a wage hike turned violent.

Asha Bhosle funeral: Ranveer Singh arrives, Mohammed Siraj consoles ZanaiAsha Bhosle Last Rites Live Updates: Legendary Indian singer Asha Bhosle died at 92 in Mumbai on Sunday, April 12, leaving behind an unparalleled legacy of music. Her funeral is set to take place at Shivaji Park at 4 pm on April 13.
Iran's choke point trump card vs Trump's counter-blockade: Who will win?The US sets the stage for an armed maritime confrontation with Iran near the Strait of Hormuz.

Saaz, the film that triggered talk around Asha Bhosle's bond with Lata didiSai Paranjpye's 1998 film, Saaz, traced the relationship between two sisters who become successful singers. Were Aruna Irani and Shabana Azmi's roles inspired by Lata Mangeshkar and Asha Bhosle in any way?

Her legacy will live on for generations: Brett Lee's tribute to Asha BhosleBrett Lee paid a heartfelt tribute to Asha Bhosle after her passing, remembering her warmth beyond her legendary voice. Reflecting on their collaboration, he said her legacy will live on for generations and continue to inspire countless artists worldwide.

How fufa ji helped make biryani fluffy, India earn Rs 25,000 crore a yearPusa Basmati 1121, India's most successful aromatic rice variety, was developed by agricultural scientist Vijaypal Singh, who followed his farmer fufa ji's (uncle's) 10-point checklist on what true Basmati should be like. The Basmati variety, which took 20 years to be developed, earns India Rs 25,000 crore annually.

Fake gun, Rs 50,000 ransom: Bengaluru students kidnap classmate to fund lavish lifeA group of college students in Bengaluru allegedly kidnapped their own classmate using a fake gun, demanding Rs 50,000 to fund a lavish lifestyle.

You can earn in lakhs selling feet photos, but is it legal in India?People are making serious money online selling something as simple as feet photos. It may sound bizarre at first, but it's happening. So, can you also turn this into a side hustle?

Trump calls Pope Leo terrible over remarks on Iran war, he hits backDonald Trump launched a blistering personal attack on Pope Leo, calling him "weak on crime" and "terrible for foreign policy" while accusing him of being soft on Iran, and even claiming the pontiff owed his position to Trump's presidency.

Dhurandhar's Aditya Uppal recalls watching Sanjay Dutt on VCR, then acting with himActor Aditya Uppal recalled watching Sanjay Dutt on VCR and shared his experience of acting with him in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He also named Farhan Akhtar as his dream collaborator.

Live: Lebanon wants ceasefire deal with Israel through direct talksUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Talks between Iran and the US faltered on Sunday after US Vice President JD Vance said that Tehran did not accept the US's terms to end the war. Vance left for the US after the talks hit a roadblock. Iran, on the other hand, dubbed the talks a failure and alleged that the US tried to demand excessively. The first phase of US-Iran talks in Islamabad concluded with both sides exchanging written texts to confirm agreements, even as earlier face-to-face meetings marked the start of direct negotiations. Limited progress was reported on key issues, including possible restrictions on strikes in southern Lebanon and movement on unfreezing Iranian assets. Iran has maintained hard red lines, insisting on a Lebanon ceasefire and broader conditions, while tensions remain high with US mine-clearing operations underway in the Strait of Hormuz and warnings issued to American vessels.

sports.headline: MI sweating over Rohit fitness, Mumbai star set for scans on hamstring issue Rohit Sharma is set to undergo scans to determine the extent of the hamstring injury he suffered during MI's loss to RCB at the Wankhede stadium on Sunday, April 12. Rohit suffered the issue during the sixth over of MI's chase.

Women's T20 World Cup: ICC announces record prize pool, winners to get USD 2.34 mn With just 60 days remaining until the start of the 2026 ICC Women's T20 World Cup, the International Cricket Council has announced a record prize money pool for the tournament, which will be held in England and Wales.

Guwahati groundstaff rewarded Rs 25 lakh for incredible job in rain-hit games The Guwahati leg of the IPL was completed despite persistent rain threats, thanks to the efforts of the groundstaff. The Assam Cricket Association rewarded the curators and staff with Rs 25 lakh for ensuring matches went ahead smoothly.

Formula 1 will return to India in 2027: Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya India is pushing hard for Formula 1's return, but the road back remains complicated. While Mansukh Mandaviya is confident of 2027, the final call still rests with Formula One.

Neymar gets World Cup boost as Carlo Ancelotti keeps Brazil door open Neymar received a timely boost in his bid to make Brazil's FIFA World Cup squad, as head coach Carlo Ancelotti insisted that the door remains open for the veteran forward.

Sachin Tendulkar breaks down in tears during Asha Bhosle's last rites in Mumbai Sachin Tendulkar was seen in tears during Asha Bhosle's last rites in Mumbai, as a viral clip captured his emotional moment with her family. The visuals reflected their close, long-standing bond beyond public life and shared admiration.

48 years later…Sawan Barwal outruns the legend of Shivnath Singh Sawan Barwal's dramatic finish at the Rotterdam Marathon did more than break a 48-year-old national record. It lifted a psychological weight that had long hung over Indian distance running. In surpassing Shivnath Singh's mark, Barwal has opened the door for a new generation to push the limits further.

Her legacy will live on for generations: Brett Lee's tribute to Asha Bhosle Brett Lee paid a heartfelt tribute to Asha Bhosle after her passing, remembering her warmth beyond her legendary voice. Reflecting on their collaboration, he said her legacy will live on for generations and continue to inspire countless artists worldwide.

Candidates: Vaishali's slip-up throws title race open, will she hold her nerve? Candidates 2026: Vaishali Rameshbabu had a golden chance to cement her lead and head into the final rest day of the Candidates 2026 tournament. But her 12th-round loss has ensured that there is everything to play for in the last two rounds, and her chances of featuring in the World Chess Championship have taken a bit of a jolt.

IPL a movie? PSL batter's remark leaves Jacob Oram in splits at presser Jacob Oram was left in splits during a Pakistan Super League press conference after being asked about Rilee Rossouw's recent remark that the IPL is "more of a movie than actual cricket," struggling to hold back his laughter at the comparison.

Why is Jasprit Bumrah not taking wickets, and should Mumbai Indians be worried? Jasprit Bumrah's 122-ball wicketless stretch in the Indian Premier League has caused concern among the Mumbai Indians fans. What's causing it? We at India Today, took a deepdive.

Can Italy still qualify for World Cup? Iran-US feud sparks hope for Gli AzzuriItaly's World Cup hopes may be hanging by a thread, but an unexpected global twist has kept the door slightly open. With uncertainty around Iran, the Azzurri are suddenly back in the conversation.

Struggling Sunrisers brace for Sooryavanshi challenge against in-form RoyalsIPL 2026, SRH vs RR: Rajasthan Royals have won all their matches thus far and will be looking to carry their winning run when they face an indifferent-looking Sunrisers Hyderabad on Monday.

Should MI play Rutherford ahead of out-of-form Tilak? RCB loss raises questionsIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians' team balance is under the spotlight after another setback, with Tilak Varma's form raising questions. Even as calls grow for change, Sherfane Rutherford has backed the top order to deliver.

Tall boy, tall hopes: Ayush Shetty's run in China shows he is here to stayBadminton Asia Championships 2026: At 6ft 4in, Ayush Shetty is no longer just a prospect. His sensational run in China proved he belongs at the top, signalling his arrival as a serious contender.

Phil Salt not overthinking IPL 2026 form: Focused on impact over milestonesIPL 2026: Phil Salt's return to form was not about numbers, but about impact when it mattered most. After a slow start, his Wankhede blitz showed exactly why RCB back him at the top.

One Pandya had to win: Krunal relishing RCB's win over MI on Wankhede homecomingIPL 2026: Krunal Pandya's Wankhede homecoming turned into a statement night as he got the better of brother Hardik. In a high-scoring clash, his control with the ball made the difference for RCB.

IPL Play of the Day: Lessons learnt as RCB's Suyash Sharma fuels Wankhede conquestIPL 2026: RCB's smarter use of Impact Subs keeps their bowling intact as Suyash Sharma's attacking spell breaks MI's chase and powers a statement win at Wankhede

Virat Kohli takes an autorickshaw on a Mumbai highway meant for FerrarisIPL 2026, MI vs RCB: Virat Kohli found himself out of sync during a run-fest in Mumbai on Sunday. While his teammates struck at 200-plus, the King's 38-ball fifty left him frustrated.

RCB namma Sinhagulu, Lions of Bengaluru: Vijay Mallya reacts after Wankhede winVijay Mallya praised RCB after their win over MI at Wankhede, where strong batting performances and key wickets in the middle overs helped secure a comfortable victory.

Hardik Pandya says Mumbai Indians will explore their options after poor start in IPLHardik Pandya admits MI are struggling to keep up, saying changes will be explored after another defeat, with issues in both batting and bowling hurting their start.

Vaishali suffers untimely loss to leave women's Candidates title race wide openR Vaishali loses to Jiner Zhu in Round 12 as the Women's Candidates 2026 title race opens up, with both players tied at the top. Divya Deshmukh's loss meant that she misses out on the chance to have a podium spot at the Candidates.

Virat Kohli injured? RCB skipper Rajat Patidar first reaction after MI winRajat Patidar provided an update on Virat Kohli's injury after the RCB star didn't turn up to field for his side during the second innings of the win against MI at the Wankhede stadium on Sunday, April 12.

Salt, Patidar lead RCB's rock and roll show at Wankhede to leave Mumbai reelingPhil Salt and Rajat Patidar's destructive batting and a disciplined bowling performance ensured that RCB returned to winning ways while piling on the pressure on Mumbai Indians. MI struggled to keep up with the momentum in the run-chase despite some late heroics from Sherfane Rutherford.

Manchester City step closer to Arsenal in PL title race, with crushing 3-0 vs ChelseaManchester City thrash Chelsea 3-0 to boost title hopes, closing gap on Arsenal, while Tottenham sink deeper into relegation trouble after defeat to Sunderland.

More than style of play, Tottenham need to change their mentality: De ZerbiRoberto De Zerbi urges mentality shift at Tottenham after defeat to Sunderland extends winless run to 14 games, leaving Spurs in deep relegation trouble with six matches remaining.

Jannik Sinner beats Carlos Alcaraz in Monte Carlo final to return as World No.1Jannik Sinner beats Carlos Alcaraz in Monte Carlo final to win his first clay Masters title, reclaim world No.1 and extend his dominant run with a fourth straight Masters 1000 crown.

MI vs RCB: Rohit Sharma walks off injured after pulling his hamstringMI vs RCB, IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma was forced to leave the field after picking up an injury during their chase against the Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai.

IPL 2026: Bengaluru and New Chandigarh emerge frontrunners to host playoffsBengaluru and New Chandigarh have emerged as the frontrunners to host the IPL 2026 playoffs, sources have revealed to India Today on Sunday, April 12.

Virat Kohli throws helmet, gloves in frustration after slow fifty vs MI: VideoIPL 2026, MI vs RCB: Virat Kohli looked frustrated after being dismissed by Hardik Pandya immediately after bringing up a 37-ball fifty against the Mumbai Indians on Sunday at the Wankhede Stadium.

RCB, MI stars pay homage to Asha Bhosle at Wankhede, sport black armbandsMumbai Indians and RCB players paid homage to the legendary Asha Bhosle ahead of their game at the Wankhede stadium on Sunday, April 12. Both teams are wearing black armbands to remember the iconic singer.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
